records.chapter: Guarding secrets ensures self-control
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 162. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: He who guards his secrets retains control in his own hands.
records.arabicText: ١٦٢. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَنْ كَتَمَ سِرَّهُ كَانَتِ الْخِيرَةُ بِيَدِهِ.

records.chapter: Disobeying human orders that contradict divine commands
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 165. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: There should be no obeying anyone against Allāh’s commands.
records.arabicText: ١٦٥. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): لَا طَاعَةَ لِمخْلُوقٍ فِي مَعْصِيَةِ الْخَالِقِ.

records.chapter: Approaching the Day of Judgement and fleeting companionship
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 168. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: The Day of Judgement is near and our mutual company is short.
records.arabicText: ١٦٨. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): الْأَمْرُ قَرِيبٌ وَالِْاصْطِحَابُ قَلِيلٌ.

records.chapter: Single eating prevents multiple cravings
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 171. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Many a single eating prevents several eatings. [1]
records.arabicText: ١٧١. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): كَمْ مِنْ أَكْلَةٍ مَنَعَتْ أَكَلاَتٍ.

records.chapter: Facing fears outweighs avoidance
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 175. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: When you are afraid of something dive straight into it, because the intensity of abstaining from it is greater (worse) than what you are afraid of.
records.arabicText: ١٧٥. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): إِذَا هِبْتَ أَمْراًفَقَعْ فِيهِ، فَإِنَّ شِدَّةَ تَوَقِّيهِأَعْظَمُ مِمَّا تَخَافُ مِنْهُ.

records.chapter: Eradicating evil by purifying one's own heart
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 178. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Cut away evil from the chest of others by snatching (it) away from your own chest. [1]
records.arabicText: ١٧٨. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): احْصُدِ الشَّرَّ مِنْ صَدْرِ غَيْرِكَ بِقَلْعِهِ مِنْ صَدْرِكَ.

records.chapter: Neglect leads to shame, foresight ensures safety
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 181. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: The result of neglect is shame, while the result of far-sightedness is safety.
records.arabicText: ١٨١. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): ثَمَرَةُ التَّفْرِيطِ النَّدَامَةُ، وَثَمَرَةُ الْحَزْمِ السَّلاَمَةُ.

records.chapter: Amīr al-mu’minīn's declaration of truth and steadfastness
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 185. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: I have neither spoken a lie nor have I been told a lie. I have neither deviated nor have I been made to deviate (others).
records.arabicText: ١٨٥. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَا كَذَبْتُ وَلَا كُذِّبْتُ، وَلَا ضَلَلْتُ وَلَا ضُلَّ بِي.

records.chapter: Turning from right leads to ruin
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 188. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Whoever turned away from right was ruined.
records.arabicText: ١٨٨. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَنْ أَبْدى صَفْحَتَهُ لِلْحَقِّ هَلَكَ.

records.chapter: Caliphate legitimacy through kinship and companionship
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 190. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: How strange? Could the caliphate be through the (Prophet’s) companionship but not through (his) companionship and (his) kinship?
records.arabicText: ١٩٠. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): وَاعَجَبَاهُ! أَتَكُونُ الْخِلاَفَةَ بِالصَّحَابَةِ وَلاَتَكُونُ بِالصَّحَابةِ وَالْقَرَابَةِ؟

records.chapter: Caliphate legitimacy through kinship and companionship
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: As-Sayyid ar-Raḍī says: Verses have also been related from Amīr al-mu’minīn on the same matter. They are:- If you claim to have secured authority by consultation, how did it happen when those to be consulted were absent! If you have scored over your opponents by kinship then someone else has greater right for being nearer to the Holy Prophet. [1]
records.arabicText: قال السيد الرضى: و روي له شعر في هذا المعنى، وهو: فَإِنْ كُنْتَ بِالشُّورَى مَلَكْتَ أُمُورَهُمْ * فَكَيْفَ بِهذَا وَالْمُشِيرُونَ غُيَّبُ وَإِنْ كنْتَ بِالْقُرْبَى حَجَجْتَ خَصِيمَهُمْ * فَغَيْرُكَ أَوْلَى بِالنَّبِيِّ وَأَقْرَبُ

records.chapter: Choosing between revenge and forgiveness when angry
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 194. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, used to say: If I am angry when shall I vent my anger - when I am unable to take revenge and it be said to me, “better you endure” or when I have power to take revenge and it be said to me, “better forgive”?
records.arabicText: ١٩٤. وكان (عليه ‏السلام) يقول: مَتَى أَشْفِي غَيْظِي إِذَا غَضِبْتُ؟ أَحِينَ أَعْجِزُ عَنِ الِانْتِقَامِ فَيُقَالُ لِي: لَوْ صَبَرْتَ؟ أَمْ حِينَ أَقْدِرُ عَلَيْهِ فَيُقَالُ لي: لَوْ عَفَوْتَ.

records.chapter: Wealth as a source of wisdom
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 196. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: The wealth that teaches you lesson does not go waste. [1]
records.arabicText: ١٩٦. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): لَمْ يَذْهَبْ مِنْ مَالِكَ مَا وَعَظَكَ.

records.chapter: Crowd dynamics in assembly and dispersal
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 199. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said about the crowd of people: These are the people who, when they assemble together, are overwhelming but when they disperse they cannot be recognized.
records.arabicText: ١٩٩. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام) فِي صِفَةِ الْغَوْغَاءِ هُمُ الَّذِينَ إِذَا اجْتَمَعُوا غَلَبُوا، وَإِذَا تَفَرَّقُوا لَمْ يُعْرَفُوا.

records.chapter: Crowd dynamics in assembly and dispersal
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: It is related that instead of this Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: These are the people who when they assemble together cause harm but when they disperse are beneficial. It was pointed out to him: We know their harm at the time of their assembling but what is their benefit at the time of their dispersal? Then he replied: The workers return to their work and people get benefit out of them, like the return of the mason to the building site, that of the weaver to his loom, and that of the baker to his bakery.
records.arabicText: وَقِيلَ بَلْ قَالَ (عليه السلام): هُمُ الَّذِينَ إِذَا اجْتَمَعُوا ضَرُّوا، وَإِذَا تَفَرَّقُوا نَفَعُوا. فقيل: قَدْ عَرَفْنَا مَضَرَّةَ اجْتِمَاعِهِمْ، فَمَا مَنْفَعَةُ افْتِرَاقِهِمْ؟ فَقَالَ: يَرْجِعُ أَصْحَابُ الْمِهَنِ إِلَى مِهَنِهِمْ، فَيَنْتَفِعُ النَّاسُ بِهِمْ، كَرُجُوعِ الْبَنَّاءِ إِلَى بِنَائِهِ، وَالنَّسَّاجِ إِلَى مَنْسَجِهِ، وَالْخَبَّازِ إِلَى مَخْبَزِهِ.

records.chapter: Divine protection and predestined fate
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 201. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: With every individual there are two angels who protect him; when destiny approaches they let it have its own way with him. Certainly, the appointed time is a protective shield (against the events which occur before it).
records.arabicText: ٢٠١. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): إِنَّ مَعَ كُلِّ إِنْسَانٍ مَلَكَيْنِ يَحْفَظَانِهِ، فَإِذَا جَاءَ الْقَدَرُ خَلَّيَا بَيْنَهُ وَبَيْنَهُ، وَإِنَّ الْأَجَلَ جُنَّةٌ حَصِينَةٌ.

records.chapter: Divine omniscience and inevitability of death
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 203. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: O people, fear Allāh Who is such that when you speak He hears and when you conceal (a secret) He knows it. Prepare yourself to meet death which will overtake you even if you run away, catch you even if you stay and remember you even if you forget it.
records.arabicText: ٢٠٣. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ، اتّقُوا اللهَ الَّذِي إِنْ قُلْتُمْ سمِعَ، وَإِنْ أَضْمَرْتُمْ عَلِمَ، وَبَادِرُوا الْمَوْتَ الَّذِي إِنْ هَرَبْتُمْ (مِنْهُ) أَدْرَكَكُمْ، وَإِنْ أَقَمْتُمْ أَخَذَكُمْ، وَإِنْ نَسِيتُمُوهُ ذَكَرَكُمْ.

records.chapter: Persisting in good deeds despite ingratitude
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 204. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: If someone is not grateful to you, that should not prevent you from good actions, because (possibly) such a person will feel grateful about it who has not even drawn any benefit from it, and his gratefulness will be more than the ingratitude of the denier; And Allāh loves those who do good. (Qur’ān, 3:134,148; 5:93)
records.arabicText: ٢٠٤. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): لَا يُزَهِّدَنَّكَ فِي الْمَعْرُوفِ مَنْ لَا يَشْكُرُهُ لَكَ، فَقَدْ يَشْكُرُكَ عَلَيْهِ مَنْ لَا يَسْتَمْتِعُ بِشَيْءٍ مِنْهُ، وَقَدْ تُدْرِكُ مِنْ شُكْرِ الشَّاكِرِ أَكْثَرَ مِمَّا أَضَاعَ الْكَافِرُ ﴿وَاللهُ يُحِبُّ الْمُحْسِنِينَ﴾.

records.chapter: Feigning forbearance to assimilate virtues
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 207. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: If you cannot forbear, feign to do so because it is seldom that a man likens himself to a group and does not become as one of them. [1]
records.arabicText: ٢٠٧. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): إِنْ لَمْ تَكُنْ حَلِيماً فَتَحَلَّمْ، فَإِنَّهُ قَلَّ مَنْ تَشَبَّهَ بَقَوْمٍ إِلَّا أَوْشَكَ أَنْ يَكُونَ مِنْهُمْ.

records.chapter: The world's eventual submission to divine authority
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 209. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him said: The world will bend towards us after having been refractory as the biting she-camel bends towards its young. Then Amīr al-mu’minīn recited the verse: And intend We to bestow (Our) favour upon those who were considered weak in the land, and to make them the Imams (guides in faith), and to make them the heirs. (Qur’ān, 28:5) [1]
records.arabicText: ٢٠٩. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): لَتَعْطِفَنَّ الدُّنْيَا عَلَيْنَا بَعْدَ شِمَاسِهَا عَطْفَ الضَّرُوسِ عَلَى وَلَدِهَا. وَتَلَا عَقِيبَ ذَلِكَ: ﴿وَنُرِيدُ أَن نَّمُنَّ عَلَى الَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَنَجْعَلَهُمْ أَئِمَّةً وَنَجْعَلَهُمُ الْوَارِثِينَ﴾.

records.chapter: Vanity as an enemy of intelligence
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 212. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: A man’s vanity for himself is one of the enemies of his intelligence. [1]
records.arabicText: ٢١٢. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): عُجْبُ الْمَرْءِ بِنَفْسِهِ أَحَدُ حُسَّادِ عَقْلِهِ.

records.chapter: Opposition undermines wise counsel
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 215. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Opposition destroys good counsel.
records.arabicText: ٢١٥. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): الْخِلاَفُ يَهْدِمُ الرَّأْيَ.

records.chapter: Jealousy reveals flawed friendship
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 218. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Jealousy by a friend means defect in his love.
records.arabicText: ٢١٨. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): حَسَدُ الصَّدِيقِ مِنْ سُقْمِ الْمَوَدَّةِ.

records.chapter: Worst provision is oppressing others
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 221. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: The worst provision for the Day of Judgement is high-handedness over people.
records.arabicText: ٢٢١. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): بِئْسَ الزَّادُ إِلَى الْمَعَادِ الْعُدْوَانُ عَلَى الْعِبَادِ.

records.chapter: Virtues and benefits of silence, justice, generosity, humility, hardship, leadership, and forbearance
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 224. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Excess of silence produces awe; justice results in more close friends; generosity hightens position; with humility blessings abound in plenty; by facing hardships leadership is achieved; by just behaviour the adversary is overpowered; and with forbearance against a fool there is increase of one’s supporters against him.
records.arabicText: ٢٢٤. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): بِكَثْرَةِ الصَّمْتِ تَكُونُ الْهَيْبَةُ وَبِالنَّصَفَةِ يَكْثُرُ الْمُوَاصِلُونَ وَبِالْإِفْضَالِ تَعْظُمُ الْأَقْدَارُ وَبِالتَّوَاضُعِ تَتِمُّ النِّعْمَةُ وَبِاحْتِمَالِ الْمُؤَنِ يَجِبُ السُّؤْدُدُ وَبِالسِّيرَةِ الْعَادِلَةِ يُقْهَرُ الْمُنَاوِئُ وَبِالْحِلْمِ عَنِ السَّفِيهِ تَكْثُرُ الْأَنْصَارُ عَلَيْهِ.
